title: 8 Best Herbal Tinctures for Treating Strep Throat Naturally

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cichoric acid helps to fight strep throat because it has antimicrobial properties that reduce the growth of the Streptococcus bacteria that cause the infection.
Alkylamides help to reduce inflammation in the throat caused by strep throat, and also have antimicrobial properties to fight off the infection and promote healing.
Polyphenols are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, fight infection, and promote healing.
Flavonoids are good for strep throat because they have strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off the infection causing the throat pain.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help fight the bacterial infection and reduce inflammation in the throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture has been used by some people to help with strep throat. The roots and flowers of the Echinacea plant are rich in compounds that may help to reduce inflammation in the throat, making it a popular natural remedy. Some of these compounds, like alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown in studies to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This means they can help to soothe and calm the sore, red tissue in the throat caused by the strep bacteria. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tincture may also have antimicrobial properties, which could help to fight off the strep infection and reduce the severity of symptoms like a sore throat and fever. Some people use the tincture as a preventative measure, taking it at the first sign of illness, to see if it can help to ward off the infection.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea pallida herbal tincture that helps with strep throat, you'll need some fresh or dried root of the plant, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like vegetable oil or grapeseed oil, and high-proof vodka or another high-proof spirit. First, dry the Echinacea pallida root, either by air-drying it or by using a food dehydrator, to prevent mold or bacteria from growing. Next, chop the dried root into small pieces and fill the glass jar about 1/4 of the way full. Then, pour the carrier oil over the root pieces, making sure they're completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Now, add the high-proof vodka or spirit to the infused oil, following the general rule of 1 part plant material to 2-3 parts liquid, and shake the jar well to combine. Label the jar with the date, the ingredients, and any instructions, and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take a few drops under your tongue, usually 2-3 times a day, and let it dissolve before swallowing.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for strep throat

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the infection in the throat.
Saponins are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, and also boost the immune system to fight off the infection.
Polyphenols are good for strep throat because they have strong antibacterial properties that can help fight the infection caused by Group A Streptococcus bacteria.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and fight off the bacteria that cause the infection.
Iridoid glycosides are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia tincture is made from a plant that helps boost the immune system. When you have strep throat, your body is fighting an infection. This tincture may help your body fight the infection faster. It has properties that reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, making it feel less painful. It also has antimicrobial properties that help kill the bacteria causing the infection. This tincture may also help reduce the severity and duration of symptoms like fever and sore throat. Some people take it to prevent getting strep throat, as it may help keep the immune system strong and ready to fight infections.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Echinacea pallida herbal tincture that helps with strep throat, you'll need to gather some fresh or dried Echinacea pallida roots and flowers. First, you need to prepare the herbs by cleaning and drying them. If using fresh herbs, cut them into small pieces and let them air-dry for a few days. If using dried herbs, skip this step. Next, you'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a dark place to store it, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Fill the jar about 2/3 full with the Echinacea pallida herbs, then pour the solvent over the herbs, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part herbs to 2 parts solvent, but you can adjust this to your liking. Close the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs release their properties into the solvent. After the infusion time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Now your Echinacea pallida tincture is ready to use - take 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, to help soothe sore throats and boost your immune system. Keep in mind that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have a severe case of strep throat or are taking any medications.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal tinctures for strep throat

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for strep throat because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and fight off the infection.
Flavonoids are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, while also boosting the immune system to fight off the infection.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture, also known as licorice root, is used for treating strep throat due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Strep throat, caused by the bacteria Group A Streptococcus, can lead to severe pain and swelling in the throat. Glycyrrhiza glabra contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and soothes the throat. This makes it easier to swallow and reduces discomfort. The tincture may also help reduce the severity and duration of the infection. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacteria causing the infection. Some studies also suggest that glycyrrhizin may help reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the pain and swelling associated with strep throat. When taken as a tincture, Glycyrrhiza glabra can be an effective natural remedy to alleviate the symptoms of strep throat.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea pallida herbal tinctures that help with strep throat, start by gathering clean, dry roots of Echinacea pallida plants. You can purchase them from a reputable herbal supplier or grow your own in your garden. Next, chop the roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar. The general ratio for making a tincture is one part herbs to one part solvent, so add an equal amount of high-proof vodka, such as 80-proof, to the jar. Make sure the roots are completely covered by the liquid. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs infuse into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Label the bottle with the date, herbal name, and the percentage of the solvent (in this case, 50% Echinacea pallida and 50% vodka). Store the tincture in the fridge to preserve its potency and use it as directed on the label, typically by taking 20-30 drops under the tongue several times a day to help alleviate symptoms of strep throat.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for strep throat

Shogaol is good for strep throat because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce swelling, fight off the infection, and ease the pain and discomfort caused by the strep bacteria.
Zingiberene is good for strep throat because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the swelling and pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tincture, also known as ginger tincture, can help with strep throat symptoms. The active compounds in ginger, like gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the throat. When you have strep throat, your throat can become sore and inflamed, making it hard to swallow and talk. Ginger tincture can help ease this discomfort by reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection. Ginger also has antimicrobial properties that can help kill the bacteria that cause strep throat, such as group A streptococcus. Taking ginger tincture can help alleviate symptoms like fever, headache, and fatigue that often come with strep throat. Additionally, ginger can help soothe the throat and calm the digestive system, which can become upset when you have strep throat.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea pallida tincture, you'll need fresh or dried roots of the plant. Harvest the roots in the fall, after the plant has gone to seed. Clean the roots thoroughly and chop them into small pieces. Fill a glass jar with the chopped roots, covering them completely with a high-proof alcohol like vodka or rum. The ratio of roots to alcohol is usually 1 part roots to 2 parts alcohol. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called maceration, and it helps the active compounds from the roots to infuse into the alcohol. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place, away from children and pets. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of strep throat, such as sore throat and fever.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for strep throat

Tannins help to reduce the severity and duration of strep throat by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the throat, which in turn speeds up the healing process.

Triterpenoids are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the throat, making them a natural remedy to alleviate symptoms of this infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tincture is a herbal remedy that can help with strep throat. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to boost the immune system and fight off infections. The tincture is made from the roots of the Astragalus plant, which contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. When taken, these compounds can help to reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak. Astragalus membranaceus tincture also contains antimicrobial properties that can help to kill the bacteria that cause strep throat, such as group A streptococcus. By helping to fight off the infection and reduce inflammation, Astragalus membranaceus tincture can help to alleviate the symptoms of strep throat, such as sore throats, fever, and swollen lymph nodes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea pallida herbal tincture for strep throat relief, you'll need fresh or dried roots of the plant, a clean glass jar, vodka or a glycerin, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. First, harvest the roots in the fall after the plant has gone to seed, and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris. Next, chop the roots into small pieces and fill the glass jar about 2/3 full. Pour enough vodka or glycerin over the roots to cover them completely, then seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the liquid extract the plant's properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Label the bottle with the date and the plant's name, and store it in a cool, dark place, where it can be used as needed to help soothe and calm strep throat symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tincture, also known as elderberry tincture, is often used to help with strep throat symptoms. The tincture is made from the flowers and berries of the Sambucus nigra plant. It contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and talk. The tincture can also help to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the infection that causes strep throat. When taken at the first sign of symptoms, the tincture may help to shorten the duration and severity of the infection. Some people also use it to help reduce fever and alleviate coughing. However, it's essential to note that while Sambucus nigra tincture may be beneficial, it's not a replacement for medical treatment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea pallida herbal tincture that helps with strep throat, you will need dried Echinacea pallida roots and flowers, a clean glass jar with a lid, a measuring cup, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Start by measuring out one part of dried Echinacea pallida roots and flowers, and combine them in the glass jar. Then, measure out two parts of the solvent, and pour it over the herbs in the jar, making sure that the herbs are completely covered. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the good properties from the herbs. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. Your Echinacea pallida tincture is now ready to use, and you can take it by mixing a few drops with water or another liquid to help soothe a sore throat and fight off strep throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe sore throats caused by strep throat. The tincture is made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, which contain powerful compounds that fight infection and reduce inflammation. When taken as a tincture, eucalyptus globulus can help kill the streptococcus bacteria that cause strep throat, reducing the severity of symptoms like pain, swelling, and fever. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptus globulus also help reduce throat swelling and pain, making it easier to swallow and speak. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has a decongestant effect, which can help relieve a runny nose and sinus pressure often associated with strep throat. By using eucalyptus globulus tincture, you can help your body fight off the infection and recover from strep throat more quickly.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea pallida herbal tinctures that help with strep throat, first you'll need to prepare the herbs. Start by drying the Echinacea pallida flowers and leaves by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place. Once they're completely dry, you can grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine one part of the dried Echinacea powder with four parts of a neutral carrier oil, such as grapeseed oil, in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar tightly and place it in a warm, sunny spot for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help soothe and heal strep throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea pallida tincture is used to help fight off strep throat. This herb is rich in compounds that boost the immune system. When you have strep throat, your body's immune response is weak, making it hard to fight off the infection. Echinacea pallida tincture helps to strengthen your immune system by increasing the production of white blood cells. These cells are like the body's soldiers, fighting off the bacteria that cause strep throat. By increasing their numbers, the tincture helps to speed up the healing process and reduce the severity of symptoms. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the pain and swelling in the throat. Some people use Echinacea pallida tincture as a preventative measure, taking it before the start of the cold and flu season to help keep their immune system strong. However, it's essential to note that it's not a substitute for medical treatment. If you have a severe case of strep throat, you should still see a doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea pallida herbal tincture, which is believed to help with strep throat, you'll need fresh or dried roots of the plant, a jar, a container for storing the tincture, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, clean and chop the roots into small pieces. Then, place the chopped roots in a clean jar, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Next, pour the solvent over the roots, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part roots to 2 parts solvent. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This will allow the roots to infuse their properties into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be stored in a cool, dark place and taken as directed by a healthcare professional or according to the label.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea pallida herbal tinctures for strep throat

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful remedy for strep throat because they contain natural ingredients that can combat the infection and alleviate symptoms. Two herbs commonly used for this purpose are Echinacea purpurea and Echinacea angustifolia. Echinacea purpurea is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of symptoms and promote healing. Echinacea angustifolia, on the other hand, is known for its ability to stimulate the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. When you have strep throat, it's essential to address the issue promptly because it can lead to complications like tonsillitis, ear infections, and even kidney damage if left untreated. By treating strep throat effectively, you can prevent these complications and get back to your normal life. For example, if you're a student, you can attend classes and participate in activities without being held back by a sore throat. If you're a working professional, you can go back to work and be productive without worrying about missing deadlines or meetings. By taking care of your health and treating strep throat with herbal tinctures, you can improve your quality of life and feel more energized and focused.
supplementary_best: A combination of Echinacea purpurea, Sambucus nigra, and Glycyrrhiza glabra can help remedy strep throat. Echinacea purpurea supports the immune system by increasing white blood cell production, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections. Sambucus nigra, also known as elderberry, has antiviral properties that directly target the strep throat virus, reducing its severity and duration. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, soothes the throat and reduces inflammation, making it easier to swallow and relieving pain. This combination works well because Echinacea purpurea and Sambucus nigra target the root cause of the infection, while Glycyrrhiza glabra provides relief from symptoms. The antiviral properties of Sambucus nigra make it a crucial addition to this combination, as it helps to combat the strep throat virus directly. This synergy between the three herbs makes them a potent and effective remedy for strep throat.
supplementary_avoid: If you have strep throat, it's best to avoid several herbal tinctures. One of them is Licorice root tincture, as it can interact with certain medications and worsen blood pressure, which may be a concern if you have strep throat.  Another one to avoid is Slippery Elm tincture, as it may slow down the healing process of your strep throat by coating your throat with a protective layer that can trap bacteria and prevent your immune system from fighting the infection.  Also, avoid Goldenseal tincture, which contains a compound that can harm the kidneys and worsen strep throat symptoms.  Avoid Sage tincture as well, as it may dry out your throat, making it harder to swallow and possibly causing discomfort.  Finally, avoid Yarrow tincture, as it can cause allergic reactions and interact with blood thinners, which may be a concern if you have strep throat.
supplementary_other_remedies: When it comes to treating strep throat, some people turn to natural remedies besides herbal tinctures. One such remedy is warm salt water gargle, which helps to reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the throat. This works because salt has antibacterial properties that can help to combat the strep bacteria. To make a warm salt water gargle, dissolve one teaspoon of salt in eight ounces of warm water and gargle several times a day.  Another natural remedy is honey, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a sore throat. You can mix a spoonful of honey with warm water or tea to make a soothing drink. Honey's thick consistency and stickiness also help to coat the throat, providing a barrier against further irritation.  Slippery elm is another natural remedy that can help with strep throat. It's a type of herb that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe a sore throat. Slippery elm works by forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes in the throat, which can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort.  Apple cider vinegar is also sometimes used to help with strep throat. It has antibacterial properties that can help to combat the strep bacteria, and its acidity can help to break down mucus and reduce inflammation. To use apple cider vinegar as a remedy, mix one tablespoon with eight ounces of water and gargle several times a day.  Elderberry is another natural remedy that can help to alleviate symptoms of strep throat. It has antiviral properties that can help to combat the strep virus, and its anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat.  Ginger is also sometimes used to help with strep throat. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, and its antibacterial properties can help to combat the strep bacteria. You can make ginger tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water, or take ginger in supplement form.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with strep throat often experience other ailments like fever, headache, and body aches. These symptoms occur because the strep bacteria infect the throat and make the body feel unwell. To heal these symptoms, some medicinal herbs can be used.  Slippery elm is a herb that can help soothe sore throats. Its mucilage helps to protect and coat the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort. This can provide relief from the pain and difficulty swallowing associated with strep throat.  Ginger is another herb that can help alleviate symptoms. Its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce fever and body aches, making it easier to feel comfortable.  Echinacea is an herb that can help boost the immune system. It can reduce the severity and duration of strep throat by supporting the body's natural defenses against infection. This can help the body to fight off the strep bacteria more effectively.  Yarrow is a herb that can help to reduce fever and body aches. Its antipyretic properties help to lower body temperature and reduce discomfort.  These herbs can help to alleviate symptoms associated with strep throat, such as fever, headache, and body aches, but it is essential to consult a doctor or healthcare professional before using any new remedies.
